origin.capability_match: Hold allocated $BERA tokens subject to vesting

attack_surface.data_description: Unauthorized modification of off-chain records or pre-deployment configurations that define Investor token allocations or vesting schedules.

security_gap.gap_description: If Investor allocation or vesting data is managed in mutable off-chain systems before being finalized on-chain, it could be maliciously altered, impacting the Investor's rightful share.

actor_risk.description: Investors (Actor 8) rely on the integrity of the processes that determine and implement their vesting schedules and token allocations.

behavioral_risk.rationale: This threat assumes a vulnerability in off-chain administrative processes. Strong on-chain vesting contracts would largely mitigate this for the execution phase.

assumptions: Details of Investor allocations or vesting terms might be stored or processed in off-chain systems (e.g., spreadsheets, databases) before being implemented via smart contracts.
These off-chain systems could have vulnerabilities or insider threats allowing unauthorized data modification.
Investors may not have direct visibility into these off-chain processes to verify integrity before on-chain deployment.

mitigation.strategy: Minimize off-chain management of definitive vesting data. Utilize immutable on-chain vesting contracts. Implement strong access controls, audit trails, and regular reconciliation for any necessary off-chain data handling. Provide transparency to investors regarding their final on-chain vesting terms.

description: An attacker (e.g., a malicious insider or external party exploiting a vulnerability) tampers with off-chain records defining an Investor's (Actor 8) $BERA token allocation or vesting schedule, leading to an incorrect amount or altered release timeline when implemented on-chain.

trace_source.matched_capability: Hold allocated $BERA tokens subject to vesting
